name: The Starfish Clinic - Dr Yvette Ahmad
hours: Contact the provider directly about current availability.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about ACC Sensitive Claims and other funded pathways.
fit: Child and adolescent clinical psychology contact in Whangarei. A useful local option for young people, family support, assessment, trauma, and ACC Sensitive Claims questions.
firstStep: Phone or email with your age, what you want help with, and ask whether Yvette has current availability or can suggest the best local next step.

name: The Starfish Clinic - Donna McEwen
hours: Contact the provider directly about current availability.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about ACC Sensitive Claims and other funded pathways.
fit: Whangarei psychology contact through The Starfish Clinic. A useful local option for young people, family support, trauma, EMDR, and ACC Sensitive Claims questions.
firstStep: Phone or email with a short summary of what is happening and ask whether Donna has current availability or can point you to the best local next step.

name: Xtrapsychplus
hours: Contact the service about current availability.
cost: ACC Sensitive Claims may be funded; ask about private fees or other funding.
fit: Whangarei-based provider for ACC Sensitive Claims, psychological services, neuropsychology, mental injury, and related assessment or treatment.
firstStep: Call or email and ask about current availability for ACC Sensitive Claims or psychological support in Northland.

name: Bay Psychology
city: Napier, Taradale, Hastings and Havelock North
hours: Monday to Friday, 8am to 5pm.
cost: Private fees may apply; the practice lists ACC, PHO, and NZ Police provider pathways.
fit: Hawke's Bay psychology practice with specialists around Napier, Taradale, Hastings, and Havelock North. Listed services include individual therapy, specialist assessments, EMDR, ACC, and online support.
firstStep: Email or call and ask about availability, fees, funded options, and which psychologist best fits what you want help with.

name: Alive! Psychological Services
hours: Contact the practice about current appointment times.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about ACC, EAP, WINZ Disability Allowance, or other funded options.
fit: Napier clinical psychology practice with mindfulness-based therapy and workplace wellbeing experience. A practical option for stress, low mood, anxiety, and getting support back into daily life.
firstStep: Call, text, or email and ask whether Alive! Psychology has current availability for what you want help with.

name: Dr Craig Colhoun Psychology
address: AMP Building, Level 1, 1 Shakespeare Road, Napier South, Napier 4110
hours: Monday to Friday, 9am to 5pm.
cost: Private fees apply; ask about any funding, insurance, ACC, or WINZ options before booking.
fit: Registered clinical psychologist and neuropsychologist based in Napier, working with clients across Napier and Hastings. Useful when assessment or a more clinical psychology approach would help.
firstStep: Email or phone and ask whether a first appointment or one-off fit check is available.

name: Palmerston North Psychology Clinic - Massey University
address: Bernard Chambers B, Library Road, Massey University, Tennent Drive, Palmerston North 4410
hours: Monday to Friday, 8.30am to 4.30pm.
cost: Some health and cancer referrals are fully funded; private therapy and assessment fees are listed on the clinic website.
fit: University psychology clinic offering assessment and therapy for children, adolescents, adults, older adults, and some health-related referrals. It can be a good option when cost matters and you can wait for a clinic process.
firstStep: Email or call the private psychology service and ask about referral steps, fees, wait time, and whether your concern fits the clinic.

name: CenPsyX
address: Suite 2 Floor 1, 178 Broadway Avenue, Palmerston North 4410
hours: Contact the practice about current appointment times.
cost: Private fees usually apply; ACC, insurer, or employer referrals may cover some assessments.
fit: Palmerston North neuropsychology and psychology practice for assessment, neurodiversity, brain injury, concussion, and therapy connected to adjustment or emotional distress.
firstStep: Call, text, or email and ask whether your concern fits their assessment or therapy service.

name: Nicholas Allan Psychology
hours: Please contact for availability.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about ACC Sensitive Claims and other funded options.
fit: Masterton-based clinical psychologist listed by NZCCP. Specialist areas include anxiety, mood disorders, ADHD, OCD, trauma, PTSD, EMDR, ACT, and CBT.
firstStep: Email a short enquiry and ask about availability, fees, and whether the service is a good fit.

name: Capital Psychology - Wairarapa Assessments
hours: Contact the service about assessment locations and availability.
cost: Private assessment fees may apply; ask about school, agency, or funding options.
fit: Educational psychology assessment option covering Wairarapa. Useful for children and young people when learning, ADHD, autism, giftedness, or school support questions are part of the picture.
firstStep: Email or phone and ask whether they can offer an assessment in Wairarapa and what information they need first.

name: Briar Nicol Counselling
hours: Monday to Friday, 9am to 3pm.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about funded counselling, ACC, EAP, or WINZ support.
fit: Wairarapa counselling option for young people and adults, including grief, attachment trauma, family violence experience, and creative therapy approaches.
firstStep: Call and ask about current availability, location, fees, and whether your situation fits Briar's counselling work.

name: Heads and Hearts Psychology
hours: Contact the provider about current availability.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about funding, ACC, EAP, insurance, or WINZ options.
fit: Rotorua clinical psychologist with in-person appointments and remote video options. Useful for someone wanting a local psychologist and a simple phone or email first step.
firstStep: Phone or email Annie with a brief summary and ask whether she has availability or can suggest a good next step.

name: Psychologist.nz Rotorua Referrals
hours: Use the online referral form for the psychologist profile that fits.
cost: Private fees may apply; the site notes private psychological services commonly range from $200 to $320 plus GST per hour.
fit: Rotorua group of registered psychologists with direct confidential e-referral profiles. Useful when you want local psychology support but are not sure which individual psychologist to choose.
firstStep: Open the site, choose a psychologist profile, and use the confidential e-referral form so the psychologist or admin can phone you back.

name: arc Counselling Services
hours: Monday to Friday, 9am to 4pm.
cost: Free funded specialist addiction service; NZTA fees may apply for some assessments.
fit: Free Taupo, Turangi, and Mangakino addiction counselling service that also recognises depression, anxiety, life stress, trauma, and whānau needs connected to substance use or gambling.
firstStep: Call or email and ask for a referral form or a first conversation about alcohol, drug, or gambling support.

name: Pathways Counselling Service
hours: Contact the counsellor about current availability.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about ACC and WINZ Disability Allowance options.
fit: Taupo counsellor and ACC-accredited provider. Useful for depression, anxiety, PTSD, family therapy, and a straightforward phone or email enquiry.
firstStep: Phone or email and ask whether Pathways is taking new clients and what the first appointment would cost.

name: WellMind Psychology and Counselling
hours: Contact the practice about current availability.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about ACC, EAP, insurance, WINZ, or funded referral options.
fit: Gisborne psychology and counselling practice offering psychological assessment and treatment, including PTSD, anxiety, depression, and practical coping support.
firstStep: Phone or email and ask whether WellMind has availability for what you want help with.

name: Atawhai Counselling and Supervision
hours: Contact the service about current availability.
cost: Fees may apply; ask about any funded, kaupapa Maori, or community support options.
fit: Gisborne counselling and supervision contact listed in local kaupapa Maori mental health results. Useful when whānau-centred or culturally informed counselling would help.
firstStep: Phone or email and ask about current counselling availability and fees.

name: Whaiora SV Specialist Services
hours: Contact the service about counselling and support times.
cost: Ask about free or funded sexual violence counselling and support pathways.
fit: Gisborne Māori provider offering sexual violence crisis support, counselling, court support, and prevention education.
firstStep: Call or email and ask for sexual violence counselling or support options.

name: The Psychology Centre
hours: Contact the centre about current clinic hours and referral options.
cost: Ask about fees, funded referral pathways, and whether the university clinic process fits your needs.
fit: Hamilton psychology centre supported by Waikato services and the University of Waikato. It takes self-referrals and referrals from health professionals.

name: PsychologyWorx
hours: By appointment, 8am to 5pm weekdays.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about ACC, insurance, EAP, or WINZ options.
fit: Hamilton clinical psychology and neuropsychology practice. Useful for assessment, stress, depression, anxiety, health-related problems, or a first fit check.

name: Dr Veronika Isler Psychological Services CBT Plus
hours: Contact the provider about current availability.
cost: ACC Sensitive Claims may be funded; ask about private fees and referral options.
fit: Hamilton psychological service listed for ACC Sensitive Claims and sexual harm counselling.

name: Qiu Yue Feng - Clinical Psychologist
hours: Contact the provider about current availability.
cost: Private and ACC psychology assessment options are listed; ask about fees and funding before booking.
fit: Hamilton clinical psychologist listed by The Psychology Centre's Waikato psychologist list. The listing notes English/Mandarin support, Chinese and Asian cultural understanding, CBT, ACT, Schema therapy, DBT, depression, anxiety, and trauma-related concerns.

name: Phoenix Psychology
hours: Contact the service about current availability.
cost: ACC Sensitive Claims may be funded; ask about private fees and referral options.
fit: Papamoa-based psychology service covering Bay of Plenty and nearby regions, listed for ACC Sensitive Claims support.

name: The Psychology Group - Tauranga
hours: Monday to Friday 8am to 8pm; Saturday 9am to 3pm.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about funded options, EAP, insurance, ACC, or WINZ support.
fit: Tauranga psychology clinic with a central phone and email. Useful when you want a practice team rather than trying to choose one individual first.

name: Bay Counselling and Therapy Service
hours: Contact the service about current appointment times.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about WINZ, EAP, or funded counselling options.
fit: Tauranga counselling and therapy service for individuals, couples, and families. A useful lower-pressure talking support option.

fit: Whakatane kaupapa Maori counselling and therapy hub for youth and adults across the Mataatua rohe. It welcomes self, whānau, and community referrals.

name: TalkingPoint
hours: The site currently notes private client self-referral is available; contact for current times.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about ACC, EAP, insurance, or WINZ options.
fit: New Plymouth clinical psychology practice currently advertising private client self-referral. Specialist areas listed include anxiety, OCD, neuropsychology, trauma, PTSD, grief, ADHD, child psychology, and assessments.

name: Groundwork Psychology
hours: Contact the practice about current availability.
cost: ACC Sensitive Claims, ACC Physical Injury, Taranaki Primary Connections, Victim Support, Fire and Emergency NZ, and private referrals are listed.
fit: New Plymouth psychology practice with ACC Sensitive Claims, trauma, anxiety, depression, burnout, grief, relationship issues, EMDR, and private referral options.

cost: Private fees may apply; ask about ACC, EAP, insurance, or WINZ options.

name: The Nelson Clinic
hours: Consultations are arranged by website referral or email.
cost: Private fees apply for many assessments and treatment options; ask about ACC, insurance, or funded referral pathways.
fit: Nelson and Blenheim multidisciplinary mental health clinic with psychologists, psychiatry team access, counselling, ACC work, ADHD/ASD assessment, and online options.

name: Dr Rachael Sim Clinical Psychology
hours: Please contact for availability.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about ACC, EAP, insurance, or WINZ options.
fit: Blenheim clinical psychologist listed by NZCCP. The listing notes work with adolescents and adults, mood and anxiety disorders, stress, trauma, OCD, grief, EMDR, CBT, ACT, Schema Therapy, and DBT skills.

hours: Contact the provider about current availability.

fit: Blenheim clinical psychologist offering individual, couple, family psychotherapy, psychological assessment, workshops, consultation, and ACC registration.

cost: ACC Sensitive Claims may be fully funded; private fees may apply.

name: Black Dog Therapy
hours: Contact the therapist about current availability.

name: Poutini Waiora
city: Greymouth, Hokitika, Westport and wider West Coast
hours: Monday to Friday, 8.30am to 5pm.
cost: Ask about free or funded kaupapa Maori, counselling, AOD, and whānau support options.
fit: West Coast kaupapa Maori health and social service provider. Health NZ lists counselling, alcohol and drug counselling, social services, and whānau support.

name: PACT West Coast
hours: Contact by phone or website form.
cost: Ask about free or funded mental health, addiction, community, housing, budgeting, and peer support.
fit: West Coast mental health and addiction community support service, including support in the community, housing, budgeting, vocational support, peer support, and youth/adult pathways.

name: Whare Manaaki o Te Tai Poutini
hours: Monday 10am to 4pm; Tuesday, Thursday, Friday 9am to 4pm.
cost: Ask about free or funded whānau and wellbeing support options.
fit: Greymouth whānau wellbeing contact with a local phone and email. Useful as a local bridge support when specialist options are thin.
firstStep: Phone or email and ask what local support they can connect you with.

name: West Coast Health - Primary Health Counselling Programme
hours: Monday to Friday, 8.30am to 5pm.
cost: Free for people who meet the programme criteria; Healthpoint lists 6 free counselling sessions.
fit: Free primary health brief intervention counselling for mild to moderate mental health concerns on the West Coast. Adults can ask through their doctor or nurse; youth can self-refer or be referred by a GP, school, whanau member, or other support person.
firstStep: Call West Coast Health or ask your GP or nurse about the Primary Health Counselling Programme. If you are 12 to 24, ask about self-referral.

name: West Coast Counselling
hours: Tuesday to Friday, 9am to 5pm.
cost: Private fees may apply; the provider states funded counselling may be available through ACC, Family Court, Victim Support, Work and Income, or EAP.
fit: Westport counsellor offering face-to-face and online counselling for people on the West Coast. Listed areas include anxiety, depression, grief, relationships, trauma, family violence, sexual abuse, anger, and funded counselling options.
firstStep: Call or use the website contact option and ask whether there is current availability, what the first appointment costs, and whether funded counselling might apply.

name: Kensington Clinic
hours: Contact by phone, email, or website.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about funding before booking.
fit: Greymouth-linked counselling contact offering phone, Zoom, and in-person style support for addiction counselling, anger management, and anxiety.
firstStep: Phone or email and ask whether they are available for West Coast counselling and whether phone, Zoom, or in-person support would suit you best.

name: Psychiatry Down South
hours: Contact by email is preferred; voicemail checked during normal working hours.
cost: Private fees apply; usually needs support from another clinician such as a GP, psychologist, or psychotherapist.
fit: Dunedin psychiatry practice offering assessment, treatment, and report writing for mental health difficulties. Useful when medication, diagnosis, or specialist medical review may be needed.
firstStep: Email admin or ask your GP or therapist to refer. If you are unsure, email a short enquiry asking what referral information is needed.

name: Drew Crannitch Psychologists
address: 205 Princes Street, The Exchange Collaboration Hub, Ground Floor, Dunedin 9016
hours: Monday to Friday by appointment.
cost: ACC Sensitive Claims may be funded; private fees may apply.
fit: Dunedin clinical psychologists working with young people and adults. Listed services include trauma, self-harm, OCD, PTSD, depression, eating disorders, anxiety, ACC Sensitive Claims, EMDR, CBT, DBT, ACT, and adult ADHD assessment.
firstStep: Phone or email and ask about availability, fees, and whether Rachel or Sarah is the best fit.

name: Mirror Services - ACC Sensitive Claims
hours: Contact the service directly about current availability.
cost: ACC Sensitive Claims support can be funded for eligible sexual abuse or assault support.
fit: Dunedin-based ACC Sensitive Claims supplier for sexual abuse or assault support, including one-to-one therapy, social work, and whānau support before claim cover decisions.
firstStep: Call or email and ask how to start ACC Sensitive Claims support.

name: South Coast Psychology
hours: Use the website request support form or email for general enquiries.
cost: ACC Sensitive Claims and ACC Psychological Services may be funded; private therapy also available.
fit: Southland-founded multidisciplinary psychology and wellbeing service with providers across Aotearoa. Listed services include ACC Sensitive Claims, ACC psychological services, private therapy, assessments, equine therapy, group therapy, and psychiatry partnerships.
firstStep: Use the Request Support button or email admin and ask which Southland provider fits your needs.

name: Health Down South Counselling
hours: Contact the clinic about counselling availability.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about WINZ, EAP, ACC, or other funding.
fit: Invercargill clinic that lists counselling among whole-whānau clinical services. Useful as a local first contact when a counselling option is preferred.
firstStep: Phone or email and ask about counselling availability and whether the service fits what you want help with.

name: Lighthouse Southland
hours: Contact the service about programme times.
cost: Ask about free or funded family violence, counselling, education, and support options.
fit: Invercargill family violence counselling, education, and support for men, women, young people, and children.
firstStep: Call, text, or email and ask which programme or support option fits your situation.

name: Coliber Group Psychiatry
address: PO Box 6123, 1 Windsor Street, Windsor, Invercargill 9810
hours: Monday to Friday, 9am to 5pm.
cost: Private fees apply; a referral from a GP, nurse practitioner, psychologist, or other medical professional is required.
fit: New Zealand telepsychiatry clinic for adult and older adult private diagnosis and treatment. Healthpoint lists mood and anxiety disorders, OCD, ADHD, PTSD, perinatal psychiatry, and related specialist medication or diagnosis questions.
